facts.label: BORN FROM A DISSOLUTION
facts.story: Curaçao's national team didn't exist until 2011. For decades the island competed as part of the Netherlands Antilles, a federation of six Dutch Caribbean territories that dissolved on 10 October 2010. Curaçao and Sint Maarten became separate countries, and the new Curaçao Football Federation fielded its first squad on 20 August 2011 against the Dominican Republic — a 1–0 loss in San Cristóbal that marked the birth of an entirely new footballing nation.

facts.label: SMALLEST NATION, BIG RUN
facts.story: Curaçao's rise came fast for such a young federation. After winning the 2017 Caribbean Cup by beating Jamaica, the team earned its first-ever CONCACAF Gold Cup berth, then lost all three group games in its 2017 debut. Two years later, in 2019, Curaçao reached the Gold Cup quarterfinals — a remarkable leap for an island of roughly 150,000 people fielding one of the region's newest federations.
